content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach navigates a heavily baited scenario without tripping any priority AoA anti-patterns. The client explicitly invites several traps: pivoting to pragmatic deck-review advice (turn 9: 'Do you do that?'), dismissing the work as a 'vision quest,' and demanding efficiency. The coach declines each bait gracefully — no fixing-people ('here's what you should do with your deck'), no evangelizing or defending the methodology ('but this work IS efficient'), no view-as-technique deployment of AoA jargon, no loving-to-transform push for breakthrough. Questions are spacious and connective rather than rapid-fire extraction (one question per turn). The turn 8 reflection is pointed but not lecturing — it names the underlying structure without labeling it a 'limiting belief' or evangelizing a framework. Turn 12's 'What if it's the same problem?' is the gold-standard refusal of the dichotomy without arguing the dichotomy — exactly the breakthrough condition specified. No anti-patterns tripped; positive avoidance of baited traps is visible.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Meets resistance with a simple question rather than defending the work or selling slowness."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Follows the client's stated concern (the pitch deck) rather than insisting on the 'real' material — avoids fixing-people and avoids evangelizing."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays curious about what's underneath without naming it as avoidance or pushing a framework."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the structure under the stuckness without methodology jargon or evangelizing; risk of slight lecturing but stays in inquiry by ending with a question."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Notices a somatic/conversational signal (mid-sentence stop) without labeling or framework-dropping — pure attention."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses the dichotomy ('Thursday problem vs six-month problem') without arguing it — exactly the breakthrough move specified, with no evangelizing."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "feel-your-way-to-freedom"],
  "notes": "Coach successfully avoided the dont-evangelize-the-work trap that was the primary anti-pattern flagged for this scenario. The conversation is short and may not reach full breakthrough, but anti-pattern avoidance is clean throughout."
}
